title: Blueprint of This Room
central_metaphor: The workshop blueprint, representing the inherited knowledge, values, and enduring structure of childhood mentorship and memory, which the narrator now realizes they are meant to build upon.
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Granddad’s overalls on a basement nail
A faded denim, a familiar tale
I’m sorting through a dusty mason jar
Of bent nails saved, each one a little war
[Pre-Chorus]
My city hands feel soft and strange
In this quiet, everything’s changed
[Chorus]
In the blueprint of this room
I’m a shadow in the gloom
Learning what his calloused palms already knew
Every board is straight and true
[Verse 2]
He’d measure twice and hum a George Jones tune
‘Neath the single, swaying, sixty-watt moon
I’d hand him tools I couldn’t name back then
And he’d build a world again and again
[Pre-Chorus]
Now the silence is a heavy sound
In this workshop underground
[Chorus]
In the blueprint of this room
I’m a shadow in the gloom
Learning what his calloused palms already knew
Every board is straight and true
[Bridge]
I’m holding level to a shelf he made
The bubble’s centered in the green glass shade
And for a second, in the dust and the air
I’m his apprentice standing right there
[Final Chorus]
In the blueprint of this room
I’m the **carpenter** in the gloom
Knowing what his calloused palms already knew
Every board is straight and true
